method_atom: a one-dimensional nonlinear field theory in which a continuous field governed by a periodic (cosine) potential supports a discrete spectrum of stable topological solitons, antisolitons, and their bound states (breathers), whose excitation energies and transition conditions between these modes are quantitatively predicted by the theory with a single dimensionless coupling parameter
object_atom: an S=1/2 one-dimensional Heisenberg antiferromagnetic chain compound (KCuGaF6) in which an applied uniform magnetic field induces a transverse staggered field through a staggered g-tensor and Dzyaloshinsky-Moriya interaction, with its elementary excitations probed by high-frequency ESR under pulsed high magnetic fields
brief_reason: The soliton/breather spectrum and resonance conditions of the periodic-potential nonlinear field theory are the transferable machinery, while the field-induced-staggered-field spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic chain measured by ESR is the domain-specific magnetic object.
